name: Tunnel of Fudge Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 sticks butter or margarine, softened (14 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 12-cup bundt pan or 10-inch angel food tube pan. Dust with flour; tap out excess.
2. In a large bowl, beat together butter and sugar with an electric mixer on medium speed until light and fluffy, about 1-2 minutes. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually add 2 cups powdered sugar, beating until well blended. Stir in flour, cocoa powder, and walnuts; mix until well combined. Spoon batter into prepared pan and spread evenly.
3. Bake for 58 to 62 minutes. Since this cake has a soft tunnel of fudge, ordinary doneness tests cannot be used. Accurate oven temperature and baking time are critical. Let the cake cool upright in the pan on a rack for 1 hour; then invert onto a serving plate and let it cool completely.
4. To make glaze, combine remaining powdered sugar, cocoa powder, and milk in a small bowl until well blended. Spoon glaze over the top of the cool cake, allowing some to run down the sides. Store the cake tightly covered.
Nuts are essential for the success of this recipe.
Serves: 14-16
